subject: Re: Don't extract particles if close to the edge of the micrograph
body:
The follow-up messages were sent to my email address instead of this 
thread. TL;DR: The particle diameter Matt selected for picking was smaller 
than the actual particle, thus some positions were too close to the border.

On Wednesday, August 22, 2018 at 2:51:23 PM UTC+2, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>
> Hey Matt,
>
> were those coordinates generated by Warp? Positions closer to the border 
> than 80% of the particle diameter should be eliminated automatically. As 
> long as you adhere to the "box = 3/2 diameter" rule of thumb, no boxes 
> should touch the micrograph edge. If some do, it's a bug. However, having 
> just checked the relevant code line, I can't see a way for it to fail.
>
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
>
>
> On Wednesday, August 22, 2018 at 2:47:51 AM UTC+2, 
> matthew.belousoff@monash.edu wrote:
>>
>> Hey mate,
>>
>> Sometimes we work with very large particles which also need a large box. 
>> If a large portion of the box is outside the micrograph, reconstructions 
>> don't always work great due to no Fourier information in them as box 
>> padding isn't the greatest. Anyway, it would be cool to see an option that 
>> automates this somewhat in WARP. Perhaps a button "don't extract if on 
>> micrograph edge". I can always write a little script to ignore the 
>> particles close the micrograph edge in in the star files, but it a more 
>> user friendly option might be a good idea. Anyway, its just a suggestion. 
>> The software is great man, we are using it to stream straight off the 
>> microscope to assess how our data collections are going, its a real game 
>> changer in my opinion. Good stuff!
>>
>> cheers
>>
>> matt B
>>
>
